children.name: Pityriasis
children.description: A monotypic genus containing the Bornean Bristlehead, one of the most unusual and enigmatic passerines in Southeast Asia. It is a large, stocky forest bird with a distinctive bald, bristly red head that gives the family its common name. The species is so distinctive that was historically placed in its own family entirely, and its closest relatives remain a subject of taxonomic debate.

children.name: Pityriasis gymnocephala
children.description: A bizarre, striking bird with a bare red head covered in short bristle-like feathers, a black body, and a massive, hooked black bill, endemic to the lowland rainforests of Borneo. It lives in noisy, active groups of six to twelve birds that travel swiftly through the canopy in search of large insects, fruit, and small vertebrates. Habitat loss from deforestation makes it one of the most threatened passerines in Borneo.
